content_html:
Private, in-browser AI summarizer powered by WebGPU, WebAssembly, or your own local Ollama.

Apogee is an AI browser assistant for articles, videos, emails, and more. It runs entirely in your browser: on your GPU via WebGPU (Chrome, Edge, and other Chromium browsers) or on your CPU via WebAssembly (Firefox). No backend, no API keys, no cloud. Just install the extension and go.

For power users, Apogee also connects directly to a local Ollama instance over 127.0.0.1 to run larger models.

TL;DR: Apogee is an offline-first, private AI assistant that runs entirely in your browser, on WebGPU in Chromium browsers and on WebAssembly in Firefox, with zero cloud dependencies or API keys. It summarizes articles, YouTube videos, and PDFs, and answers questions about them using local retrieval, all with complete privacy. Power users can switch to Local Ollama mode to run larger models on their own machine, still with nothing leaving it. Apogee is designed as a fully local, privacy-respecting alternative to cloud-dependent tools like Mozilla's discontinued Orbit.

Inspiration: Orbit (Killed by Mozilla)

Apogee was inspired by Mozilla's discontinued Orbit project (read the Review of Orbit by Mozilla). Orbit attempted to provide browser-based page summarization, but it relied on centralized API servers (Mistral 7B) and cached summaries on the server side using endpoints like store_result.

Apogee fixes Orbit's architectural and privacy flaws by being fully local-first:

  • Zero Server Overhead: Instead of routing queries through remote cloud APIs, Apogee performs tokenization and inference completely on-device via WebGPU.
  • No Data Leaks: Apogee does not send page content or generated summaries to any external endpoint, your data never leaves your machine.
  • Corporate Independence: Because Apogee has no server dependencies or cloud infrastructure to pay for, it can never be shut down or sunset.

How It Works

Apogee runs quantized language models directly in your browser. On Chrome, Edge, and other Chromium browsers it uses WebLLM, which executes models on your GPU via WebGPU. On Firefox it uses Transformers.js, which runs smaller ONNX models on your CPU via WebAssembly and performs well on a modern CPU. In both cases the first use downloads the model weights (roughly 270 MB to 2.2 GB depending on the model) and caches them locally. After that, everything runs offline.

Prefer larger models? Switch to Local Ollama mode and the extension talks directly to your own Ollama instance over 127.0.0.1, with no separate backend to install or run.

Ask goes further than the summary itself: instead of blindly truncating long pages to the first few thousand characters, Apogee embeds the page locally (a small on-device model, same trust tier as the LLM weights above) and answers using only the passages most relevant to your question. This means asking about something buried deep in a long article, PDF, or video transcript still works, not just what fit in the opening truncated slice. (Retrieval currently runs on Chromium browsers; Firefox falls back to the plain truncated slice for now.)

Highlight-in-page lets you check the summary against the source: click any bullet (or line, in Sentences/Paragraphs mode) and Apogee finds the passage of the original page it's most likely grounded in using the same on-device retrieval Ask uses, then scrolls to and highlights it in the live page. Useful for spot-checking a claim without re-reading the whole article. Chromium-only for now, same constraint as Ask's retrieval above.

Quick Start

  1. Install the extension (see below).
  2. Open any webpage.
  3. Click the Apogee icon, then Summarize this page.
  4. On first use, the model downloads automatically. After that it's instant.

That's it. No backend installation, no terminal commands.

Faster ways to summarize: right-click anywhere on a page and pick Summarize this page, or use the keyboard shortcut (default Alt+Shift+U, remappable at chrome://extensions/shortcuts). Either works without opening the popup at all. A system notification lets you know when it's ready; click it (or open the popup) to see the result. If you open the popup while it's still generating, it shows the normal loading view instead of the default Home page.

Two Ways to Use Apogee

Apogee offers two modes of operation to balance ease-of-use and raw capabilities:

In-Browser AI (WebLLM on Chrome/Edge, Transformers.js on Firefox) Local Ollama
Model Size: Small, fast models (~270 MB to 2.2 GB) Model Size: Larger, more capable models (4B to 8B+)
Setup: Zero setup required; automatic download on first run Setup: Requires installing Ollama (no separate backend to run)
Execution: Runs directly in the browser, via WebGPU (WebLLM) or WebAssembly (Transformers.js) Execution: Extension talks directly to Ollama's HTTP API
Offline: Fully offline after model weights are cached Offline: Fully offline, communicating over 127.0.0.1

Supported In-Browser Models

WebLLM (WebGPU, Chrome/Edge)

Model Download Size Best For
Qwen 2.5 1.5B (default) ~900 MB Multilingual summarization
SmolLM2 1.7B ~1 GB General tasks
Llama 3.2 1B ~700 MB Lightweight, fast
Phi 3.5 Mini ~2.2 GB Stronger reasoning

Transformers.js (WASM/CPU, Firefox only)

Model Download Size Best For
SmolLM2 360M (default) ~270 MB Smallest/fastest, quick summaries
Qwen 2.5 0.5B ~480 MB Multilingual
Llama 3.2 1B ~1.2 GB Stronger reasoning, slower on CPU

Runs via Transformers.js (ONNX models on the WASM backend). Chosen specifically because it never spawns a Worker (onnxruntime-web's proxy mode is hardcoded off), unlike WebGPU-based WebLLM (needs an offscreen document Firefox doesn't have) or wllama (needs a blob:-URL Worker, which both Chrome's and Firefox's extension CSP block). Generation is single-threaded (extension pages aren't cross-origin-isolated, so no SharedArrayBuffer) and context is capped at 4096 tokens to keep latency reasonable on CPU. On a modern/fast CPU this still summarizes well with the default SmolLM2 360M model; on older or low-power hardware, expect noticeably slower generation, and consider switching to Local Ollama instead.

Supported Ollama Models

Any model you've pulled shows up automatically in the extension's settings (see Advanced: Local Ollama Mode). These are just a starting point if you haven't pulled anything yet:

Model Size Command to pull Recommended For
Gemma 3 ~4B ollama pull gemma3:4b Excellent lightweight tasks
Qwen 3 8B ~8B ollama pull qwen3:8b Multi-turn chat & reasoning
Mistral Latest ~7B ollama pull mistral:latest General language capabilities
Llama 3.1 8B ~8B ollama pull llama3.1:8b General reasoning & coding

Summarization also adapts its chunking to the model you pick: larger-context models (e.g. llama3.1, qwen2.5, gemma3) get bigger chunks and fewer passes over long content, rather than the same fixed chunk size regardless of what the model can actually handle.

Browser Support

Apogee ships two builds: a Chromium build (dist/chrome, Manifest V3 with an offscreen document for WebGPU) and a Firefox build (dist/firefox, no offscreen document). Anything Chromium-based accepts the same build.

Browser WebLLM (In-Browser AI, WebGPU) Transformers.js (In-Browser AI, WASM) Local Ollama Notes
Chrome 113+ Yes No Yes Primary target, most tested
Edge 113+ Yes No Yes Chromium-based, same engine as Chrome
Dia Yes No Yes Chromium-based
Brave Should work No Yes Chromium-based; WebGPU may need enabling in brave://flags, not independently verified
Opera / Opera GX Should work No Yes Chromium-based, not independently verified
Vivaldi Should work No Yes Chromium-based, not independently verified
Arc Should work No Yes Chromium-based, not independently verified
Firefox 140+ No Yes (default) Yes Firefox's WebExtensions implementation has no browser.offscreen API, which WebLLM needs to run WebGPU outside a visible tab (a service worker can't access WebGPU directly). Transformers.js needs neither WebGPU nor a Worker, so it runs directly in Firefox's background page instead, and is the default in-browser provider there. The Firefox build declares strict_min_version: 140.0 (needed for the manifest's data_collection_permissions key), older Firefox will refuse to install it rather than fail silently.
Safari No No Apogee doesn't currently build or ship a Safari extension (a separate packaging toolchain from Chrome/Firefox); not evaluated regardless of Safari's own WebGPU support

See MDN's WebGPU API browser compatibility table for exact per-browser/per-OS WebGPU version support, it's a fast-moving target and a better source of truth than a number hardcoded here. A GPU with WebGPU support (most GPUs from the last several years) is required for the In-Browser (WebLLM) mode specifically. Local Ollama mode has no GPU requirement of its own beyond whatever Ollama itself needs.

Install the Extension

Chrome, Edge, Brave, Opera, Vivaldi, Arc, Dia

These are all Chromium-based and use the same dist/chrome build and load steps; only the extensions-page URL differs slightly (chrome://extensions, edge://extensions, brave://extensions, dia://extensions/, etc.).

  1. Download the packaged extension .zip from Releases.
  2. Extract/unzip the downloaded .zip file on your machine.
  3. Open your browser's extensions page (chrome://extensions on Chrome/Brave/Opera/Vivaldi, edge://extensions on Edge, dia://extensions/ on Dia).
  4. Enable Developer mode (toggle in the top-right).
  5. Click Load unpacked and select the extracted folder (containing manifest.json, not the ZIP file itself).

Build from Source (Developer Option)

  1. Clone this repository.

  2. cd apogee-extension && npm install && npm run build

  3. Go to your browser's extensions page and enable Developer mode.

  4. Click Load unpacked and select the apogee-extension/dist/chrome folder.

    npm run build produces both dist/chrome and dist/firefox. Use dist/chrome for any Chromium-based browser and dist/firefox for Firefox. You can also build a single target with npm run build:chrome or npm run build:firefox.

Firefox

You can install Apogee directly from Mozilla Add-ons or download the package from Releases.

Firefox works out of the box: in-browser AI runs via Transformers.js on WebAssembly (SmolLM2 360M by default, no setup needed). On older or low-power CPUs, generation can be slow, switch to Local Ollama mode in settings for faster results with larger models.

Advanced: Local Ollama Mode

If you prefer running larger models (8B+) locally through Ollama, Apogee talks to it directly over HTTP; there's no separate backend server to install or keep running.

1. Install Ollama

Install from https://ollama.com, then pull the models you want:

ollama pull gemma3:4b   # and qwen3:8b, mistral:latest, llama3.1:8b

2. Allow the extension to reach Ollama (CORS)

Ollama only accepts browser-originated requests from an allow-listed set of origins, and chrome-extension:// isn't in that default list. Set OLLAMA_ORIGINS before starting Ollama:

OLLAMA_ORIGINS="chrome-extension://<your-extension-id>" ollama serve

Find <your-extension-id> on chrome://extensions (with Developer mode on).

If Ollama runs as a system service instead (e.g. installed via a package manager on Linux), set it as a persistent override rather than passing it on the command line:

sudo mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/ollama.service.d
printf '[Service]\nEnvironment="OLLAMA_ORIGINS=chrome-extension://<your-extension-id>"\n' \
  | sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/ollama.service.d/override.conf
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl restart ollama

Security note: OLLAMA_ORIGINS=chrome-extension://* (allowing every extension) is convenient while developing, but it means any other extension installed in your browser could also reach your local Ollama instance's API. Scope it to your specific extension ID for anything beyond quick local testing.

3. Point the extension at Ollama

Open the extension, go to Settings, and select Local Ollama. The host field defaults to http://127.0.0.1:11434 (Ollama's own default port), only change it if you've configured Ollama to listen elsewhere.

Once connected, the model list is populated live from whatever you've actually pulled (via Ollama's own /api/tags), not a fixed list, so any model you ollama pull shows up automatically. If Ollama isn't reachable yet, a small default list is shown instead so you can still pick a model before starting it.

That's it: no apogee-backend, no separate server process to manage.

Performance Benchmarks

In-Browser AI (WebGPU)

  • Generation Throughput: ~30 to 50 tokens/s (GPU dependent)
  • Model Cold-load: ~1 to 3 seconds (once cached in browser storage)
  • First-run Cache Download: ~1 to 3 minutes depending on network bandwidth (to download the ~700 MB to 2.2 GB model weights)

Local Ollama

Measured locally on an Apple M2 (gemma3:4b, GPU via Metal):

Metric Value
Generation throughput ~73 tokens/s
Model cold-load ~0.25 s
Short page / question ~1 to 1.5 s end to end
Long page (~40k chars, multi-chunk) first bullets in ~2 s, ~12 s total

Privacy & Permissions

Privacy is the core pillar of Apogee. The key guarantee is simple: your page content and the summaries/answers generated from it are never sent to any cloud service or third party. Inference happens on your own device (WebGPU) or your own machine (127.0.0.1 Ollama). The details below are precise about the few network requests that do occur and what is kept on disk.

  • Where inference happens:
    • In-Browser mode: Tokenization and inference run entirely on your local device, on the GPU via WebGPU (WebLLM, Chrome/Edge) or on the CPU via WebAssembly (Transformers.js, Firefox). Your page content and summaries are never transmitted anywhere.
    • Local Ollama mode: Page content travels exclusively over local loopback (127.0.0.1) directly to your own Ollama instance's HTTP API, never to the cloud. There is no intermediate backend process in the path, the extension is Ollama's only client-side hop.
  • The only outbound network requests Apogee makes:
    • Model weights are downloaded once from Hugging Face (in-browser mode) or pulled by Ollama (local mode), then cached and reused offline. This transfers no page content, only the model files themselves.
    • WebLLM runtime files: in-browser mode also fetches the WebLLM library's own config/wasm assets from raw.githubusercontent.com, the same as the model weights above, no page content, just the runtime itself.
    • In-browser WASM runtime: Ask's local embedding model, and Firefox's Transformers.js summarization engine, load their shared ONNX WASM runtime once from cdn.jsdelivr.net (model weights come from Hugging Face, same as above), again the runtime only, never page content.
    • YouTube transcripts: on a YouTube page, the extractor fetches that video's caption track from YouTube/Google (the site you're already on) to feed the transcript to the model. It is restricted to genuine youtube.com/google.com/googlevideo.com hosts.
    • YouTube sponsor-segment lookup (SponsorBlock): when summarizing a YouTube video, Apogee asks the crowdsourced SponsorBlock API which parts of the video are sponsor reads/self-promo, so they can be stripped from the transcript. This uses SponsorBlock's privacy-preserving k-anonymity endpoint: only the first 4 hex characters of the SHA-256 hash of the video ID are sent (never the video ID, URL, or any page content), and the matching entry is picked out locally. If the lookup fails, a local phrase heuristic runs instead, with no network call at all.
    • That's it, there are no other external calls. (See the extension's content_security_policy.connect-src in manifest.json for the exact allow-list this is enforced against, and ALLOWED_OLLAMA_HOSTS in background/service-worker.js, which rejects any Local Ollama host setting that isn't plain http://127.0.0.1 or http://localhost.)
  • PDFs: PDF text extraction runs fully client-side using pdf.js bundled into the extension, the PDF is downloaded straight into the browser tab (using that tab's own network context) and parsed there. Only the extracted text is ever handed to the model; the file itself never passes through any other process.
  • Local Ollama's CORS setting (OLLAMA_ORIGINS): for the extension to reach Ollama at all, Ollama must be told to accept requests from the extension's origin, see Advanced: Local Ollama Mode. This is a browser-enforced allow-list, not a data-transmission path, but be aware that setting it to a wildcard (chrome-extension://*) rather than your specific extension ID lets any installed extension talk to your local Ollama API, not just Apogee. Ollama itself still only binds to 127.0.0.1 by default regardless of this setting, so it's never reachable from your network either way, this only affects which browser extensions can call it.
  • No Telemetry, Tracking, or Analytics: Apogee includes no Google Analytics, Mixpanel, crash-reporting SDKs, or telemetry of any kind. No usage data is collected.
  • What's stored on your device (and how to control it):
    • To make reopening the popup instant, Apogee caches summaries, suggested prompts, extracted page text (for articles), and your recent questions/answers in local extension storage (chrome.storage.local), never transmitted, capped in size, and keyed by a hash of the URL (so URLs with tokens aren't stored in plaintext keys).
    • Sensitive sites are never cached, pages on known webmail/messaging hosts (Gmail, Outlook, Proton Mail, Yahoo Mail, Google Messages, WhatsApp Web, Telegram Web, Slack, Discord, Microsoft Teams) are always treated as ephemeral, regardless of your setting.
    • Under Settings, Privacy, you can switch to "Don't save (this session only)" so nothing page-derived is written to disk, and "Clear cached summaries & page data" wipes all cached content on demand (your preferences are kept).
  • Browser Permission Sandboxing:
    • activeTab + scripting: Apogee cannot read your browsing history or inspect other open tabs. It reads the currently active tab only when you click "Summarize"/"Ask", right-click and choose "Summarize this page", or use the keyboard shortcut.
    • storage: Holds your preferences plus the local cache described above.
    • unlimitedStorage: Lifts the default quota on chrome.storage.local so the cached summaries/page text above aren't evicted under normal storage pressure, it does not grant access to anything beyond that cache.
    • offscreen (Chrome/Edge only): Runs the in-browser WebLLM engine in a hidden document, since a service worker can't access WebGPU directly. Not used, and not requested, in the Firefox build.
    • alarms: Schedules the housekeeping timers that close the idle in-browser model and clean up finished request buffers, these need to survive the extension's background worker being suspended between uses. No user data is involved.
    • clipboardWrite: Lets the popup's copy buttons (summary, answer, past summaries) write to your clipboard directly when you click them, instead of routing through an interactive browser permission prompt. Write-only, the extension can never read your clipboard's existing contents.
    • contextMenus: Adds the "Summarize this page" right-click entry. Doesn't grant any visibility into your browsing beyond the page you right-clicked on, which activeTab already covers.
    • notifications: Shows a local OS notification when a right-click/keyboard-shortcut-triggered summary finishes or fails, so you know it's ready without needing to keep the popup open. Purely local UI, no data leaves your device to show it.
  • Model weights are stored in standard browser cache structures locally and never transmitted.

Development

cd apogee-extension
npm install
npm run dev    # watch mode, rebuilds on changes

Load the dist/chrome folder (or dist/firefox) as an unpacked extension in your browser.

Before opening a PR, see CONTRIBUTING.md for the lint/format/test/build checks CI runs.

Changelog

See CHANGELOG.md for release notes.

License

MIT

Allow the extension to reach Ollama (CORS) Ollama only accepts browser-originated requests from an allow-listed set of origins, and chrome-extension:// isn't in that default list. Set OLLAMA_ORIGINS before starting Ollama: OLLAMA_ORIGINS="chrome-extension://<your-extension-id>" ollama serve Find <your-extension-id> on chrome://extensions (with Developer mode on). If Ollama runs as a system service instead (e.g. installed via a package manager on Linux), set it as a persistent override rather than passing it on the command line: sudo mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/ollama.service.d printf '[Service]\nEnvironment="OLLAMA_ORIGINS=chrome-extension://<your-extension-id>"\n' \ | sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/ollama.service.d/override.conf sudo systemctl daemon-reload sudo systemctl restart ollama Security note: OLLAMA_ORIGINS=chrome-extension://* (allowing every extension) is convenient while developing, but it means any other extension installed in your browser could also reach your local Ollama instance's API. Scope it to your specific extension ID for anything beyond quick local testing. 3. Point the extension at Ollama Open the extension, go to Settings, and select Local Ollama. The host field defaults to http://127.0.0.1:11434 (Ollama's own default port), only change it if you've configured Ollama to listen elsewhere. Once connected, the model list is populated live from whatever you've actually pulled (via Ollama's own /api/tags), not a fixed list, so any model you ollama pull shows up automatically. If Ollama isn't reachable yet, a small default list is shown instead so you can still pick a model before starting it. That's it: no apogee-backend, no separate server process to manage. Performance Benchmarks In-Browser AI (WebGPU) Generation Throughput: ~30 to 50 tokens/s (GPU dependent) Model Cold-load: ~1 to 3 seconds (once cached in browser storage) First-run Cache Download: ~1 to 3 minutes depending on network bandwidth (to download the ~700 MB to 2.2 GB model weights) Local Ollama Measured locally on an Apple M2 (gemma3:4b, GPU via Metal): Metric Value Generation throughput ~73 tokens/s Model cold-load ~0.25 s Short page / question ~1 to 1.5 s end to end Long page (~40k chars, multi-chunk) first bullets in ~2 s, ~12 s total Privacy & Permissions Privacy is the core pillar of Apogee. The key guarantee is simple: your page content and the summaries/answers generated from it are never sent to any cloud service or third party. Inference happens on your own device (WebGPU) or your own machine (127.0.0.1 Ollama). The details below are precise about the few network requests that do occur and what is kept on disk. Where inference happens: In-Browser mode: Tokenization and inference run entirely on your local device, on the GPU via WebGPU (WebLLM, Chrome/Edge) or on the CPU via WebAssembly (Transformers.js, Firefox). Your page content and summaries are never transmitted anywhere. Local Ollama mode: Page content travels exclusively over local loopback (127.0.0.1) directly to your own Ollama instance's HTTP API, never to the cloud. There is no intermediate backend process in the path, the extension is Ollama's only client-side hop. The only outbound network requests Apogee makes: Model weights are downloaded once from Hugging Face (in-browser mode) or pulled by Ollama (local mode), then cached and reused offline. This transfers no page content, only the model files themselves. WebLLM runtime files: in-browser mode also fetches the WebLLM library's own config/wasm assets from raw.githubusercontent.com, the same as the model weights above, no page content, just the runtime itself. In-browser WASM runtime: Ask's local embedding model, and Firefox's Transformers.js summarization engine, load their shared ONNX WASM runtime once from cdn.jsdelivr.net (model weights come from Hugging Face, same as above), again the runtime only, never page content. YouTube transcripts: on a YouTube page, the extractor fetches that video's caption track from YouTube/Google (the site you're already on) to feed the transcript to the model. It is restricted to genuine youtube.com/google.com/googlevideo.com hosts. YouTube sponsor-segment lookup (SponsorBlock): when summarizing a YouTube video, Apogee asks the crowdsourced SponsorBlock API which parts of the video are sponsor reads/self-promo, so they can be stripped from the transcript. This uses SponsorBlock's privacy-preserving k-anonymity endpoint: only the first 4 hex characters of the SHA-256 hash of the video ID are sent (never the video ID, URL, or any page content), and the matching entry is picked out locally. If the lookup fails, a local phrase heuristic runs instead, with no network call at all. That's it, there are no other external calls. (See the extension's content_security_policy.connect-src in manifest.json for the exact allow-list this is enforced against, and ALLOWED_OLLAMA_HOSTS in background/service-worker.js, which rejects any Local Ollama host setting that isn't plain http://127.0.0.1 or http://localhost.) PDFs: PDF text extraction runs fully client-side using pdf.js bundled into the extension, the PDF is downloaded straight into the browser tab (using that tab's own network context) and parsed there. Only the extracted text is ever handed to the model; the file itself never passes through any other process. Local Ollama's CORS setting (OLLAMA_ORIGINS): for the extension to reach Ollama at all, Ollama must be told to accept requests from the extension's origin, see Advanced: Local Ollama Mode. This is a browser-enforced allow-list, not a data-transmission path, but be aware that setting it to a wildcard (chrome-extension://*) rather than your specific extension ID lets any installed extension talk to your local Ollama API, not just Apogee. Ollama itself still only binds to 127.0.0.1 by default regardless of this setting, so it's never reachable from your network either way, this only affects which browser extensions can call it. No Telemetry, Tracking, or Analytics: Apogee includes no Google Analytics, Mixpanel, crash-reporting SDKs, or telemetry of any kind. No usage data is collected. What's stored on your device (and how to control it): To make reopening the popup instant, Apogee caches summaries, suggested prompts, extracted page text (for articles), and your recent questions/answers in local extension storage (chrome.storage.local), never transmitted, capped in size, and keyed by a hash of the URL (so URLs with tokens aren't stored in plaintext keys). Sensitive sites are never cached, pages on known webmail/messaging hosts (Gmail, Outlook, Proton Mail, Yahoo Mail, Google Messages, WhatsApp Web, Telegram Web, Slack, Discord, Microsoft Teams) are always treated as ephemeral, regardless of your setting. Under Settings, Privacy, you can switch to "Don't save (this session only)" so nothing page-derived is written to disk, and "Clear cached summaries & page data" wipes all cached content on demand (your preferences are kept). Browser Permission Sandboxing: activeTab + scripting: Apogee cannot read your browsing history or inspect other open tabs. It reads the currently active tab only when you click "Summarize"/"Ask", right-click and choose "Summarize this page", or use the keyboard shortcut. storage: Holds your preferences plus the local cache described above. unlimitedStorage: Lifts the default quota on chrome.storage.local so the cached summaries/page text above aren't evicted under normal storage pressure, it does not grant access to anything beyond that cache. offscreen (Chrome/Edge only): Runs the in-browser WebLLM engine in a hidden document, since a service worker can't access WebGPU directly. Not used, and not requested, in the Firefox build. alarms: Schedules the housekeeping timers that close the idle in-browser model and clean up finished request buffers, these need to survive the extension's background worker being suspended between uses. No user data is involved. clipboardWrite: Lets the popup's copy buttons (summary, answer, past summaries) write to your clipboard directly when you click them, instead of routing through an interactive browser permission prompt. Write-only, the extension can never read your clipboard's existing contents. contextMenus: Adds the "Summarize this page" right-click entry. Doesn't grant any visibility into your browsing beyond the page you right-clicked on, which activeTab already covers. notifications: Shows a local OS notification when a right-click/keyboard-shortcut-triggered summary finishes or fails, so you know it's ready without needing to keep the popup open. Purely local UI, no data leaves your device to show it. Model weights are stored in standard browser cache structures locally and never transmitted. Development cd apogee-extension npm install npm run dev # watch mode, rebuilds on changes Load the dist/chrome folder (or dist/firefox) as an unpacked extension in your browser. Before opening a PR, see CONTRIBUTING.md for the lint/format/test/build checks CI runs. Changelog See CHANGELOG.md for release notes. blocks.html_en: Private, in-browser AI summarizer powered by WebGPU, WebAssembly, or your own local Ollama.

blocks.html_en: Apogee is an AI browser assistant for articles, videos, emails, and more. It runs entirely in your browser: on your GPU via WebGPU (Chrome, Edge, and other Chromium browsers) or on your CPU via WebAssembly (Firefox). No backend, no API keys, no cloud. Just install the extension and go.

blocks.html_en: TL;DR: Apogee is an offline-first, private AI assistant that runs entirely in your browser, on WebGPU in Chromium browsers and on WebAssembly in Firefox, with zero cloud dependencies or API keys. It summarizes articles, YouTube videos, and PDFs, and answers questions about them using local retrieval, all with complete privacy. Power users can switch to Local Ollama mode to run larger models on their own machine, still with nothing leaving it. Apogee is designed as a fully local, privacy-respecting alternative to cloud-dependent tools like Mozilla's discontinued Orbit.

blocks.html_en: Apogee was inspired by Mozilla's discontinued Orbit project (read the Review of Orbit by Mozilla). Orbit attempted to provide browser-based page summarization, but it relied on centralized API servers (Mistral 7B) and cached summaries on the server side using endpoints like store_result.

blocks.html_en: • Zero Server Overhead: Instead of routing queries through remote cloud APIs, Apogee performs tokenization and inference completely on-device via WebGPU.

blocks.html_en: • No Data Leaks: Apogee does not send page content or generated summaries to any external endpoint, your data never leaves your machine.

sentences.en: How It Works
sentences.zh: 它是如何运作的

blocks.html_en: Apogee runs quantized language models directly in your browser. On Chrome, Edge, and other Chromium browsers it uses WebLLM, which executes models on your GPU via WebGPU. On Firefox it uses Transformers.js, which runs smaller ONNX models on your CPU via WebAssembly and performs well on a modern CPU. In both cases the first use downloads the model weights (roughly 270 MB to 2.2 GB depending on the model) and caches them locally. After that, everything runs offline.

sentences.zh: Apogee 直接在浏览器中运行量化语言模型。

sentences.zh: 在 Chrome、Edge 和其他 Chromium 浏览器上，它使用 WebLLM，它通过 WebGPU 在 GPU 上执行模型。

sentences.en: On Firefox it uses Transformers.js, which runs smaller ONNX models on your CPU via WebAssembly and performs well on a modern CPU.
sentences.zh: 在 Firefox 上，它使用 Transformers.js，它通过 WebAssembly 在 CPU 上运行较小的 ONNX 模型，并在现代 CPU 上表现良好。

sentences.en: After that, everything runs offline.
sentences.zh: 之后，一切都离线运行。

blocks.html_en: Ask goes further than the summary itself: instead of blindly truncating long pages to the first few thousand characters, Apogee embeds the page locally (a small on-device model, same trust tier as the LLM weights above) and answers using only the passages most relevant to your question. This means asking about something buried deep in a long article, PDF, or video transcript still works, not just what fit in the opening truncated slice. (Retrieval currently runs on Chromium browsers; Firefox falls back to the plain truncated slice for now.)

sentences.en: Ask goes further than the summary itself: instead of blindly truncating long pages to the first few thousand characters, Apogee embeds the page locally (a small on-device model, same trust tier as the LLM weights above) and answers using only the passages most relevant to your question.
sentences.zh: Ask 比摘要本身更进一步：Apogee 不是盲目地将长页面截断到前几千个字符，而是在本地嵌入页面（一个小型​​设备上模型，与上面的 LLM 权重相同的信任层）并仅使用与您的问题最相关的段落进行回答。

blocks.html_en: Highlight-in-page lets you check the summary against the source: click any bullet (or line, in Sentences/Paragraphs mode) and Apogee finds the passage of the original page it's most likely grounded in using the same on-device retrieval Ask uses, then scrolls to and highlights it in the live page. Useful for spot-checking a claim without re-reading the whole article. Chromium-only for now, same constraint as Ask's retrieval above.

blocks.html_en: Runs via Transformers.js (ONNX models on the WASM backend). Chosen specifically because it never spawns a Worker (onnxruntime-web's proxy mode is hardcoded off), unlike WebGPU-based WebLLM (needs an offscreen document Firefox doesn't have) or wllama (needs a blob:-URL Worker, which both Chrome's and Firefox's extension CSP block). Generation is single-threaded (extension pages aren't cross-origin-isolated, so no SharedArrayBuffer) and context is capped at 4096 tokens to keep latency reasonable on CPU. On a modern/fast CPU this still summarizes well with the default SmolLM2 360M model; on older or low-power hardware, expect noticeably slower generation, and consider switching to Local Ollama instead.

blocks.html_en: Firefox's WebExtensions implementation has no browser.offscreen API, which WebLLM needs to run WebGPU outside a visible tab (a service worker can't access WebGPU directly). Transformers.js needs neither WebGPU nor a Worker, so it runs directly in Firefox's background page instead, and is the default in-browser provider there. The Firefox build declares strict_min_version: 140.0 (needed for the manifest's data_collection_permissions key), older Firefox will refuse to install it rather than fail silently.

blocks.html_en: Privacy is the core pillar of Apogee. The key guarantee is simple: your page content and the summaries/answers generated from it are never sent to any cloud service or third party. Inference happens on your own device (WebGPU) or your own machine (127.0.0.1 Ollama). The details below are precise about the few network requests that do occur and what is kept on disk.

sentences.en: Privacy is the core pillar of Apogee.
sentences.zh: 隐私是 Apogee 的核心支柱。

sentences.en: The key guarantee is simple: your page content and the summaries/answers generated from it are never sent to any cloud service or third party.
sentences.zh: 关键保证很简单：您的页面内容以及从中生成的摘要/答案永远不会发送到任何云服务或第三方。

sentences.en: Inference happens on your own device (WebGPU) or your own machine (127.0.0.1 Ollama).
sentences.zh: 推理发生在您自己的设备 (WebGPU) 或您自己的计算机 (127.0.0.1 Ollama) 上。

sentences.en: The details below are precise about the few network requests that do occur and what is kept on disk.
sentences.zh: 下面的详细信息是关于确实发生的少数网络请求以及磁盘上保存的内容的详细信息。

blocks.html_en:
• Where inference happens:
    • In-Browser mode: Tokenization and inference run entirely on your local device, on the GPU via WebGPU (WebLLM, Chrome/Edge) or on the CPU via WebAssembly (Transformers.js, Firefox). Your page content and summaries are never transmitted anywhere.
    • Local Ollama mode: Page content travels exclusively over local loopback (127.0.0.1) directly to your own Ollama instance's HTTP API, never to the cloud. There is no intermediate backend process in the path, the extension is Ollama's only client-side hop.

blocks.html_en:
• The only outbound network requests Apogee makes:
    • Model weights are downloaded once from Hugging Face (in-browser mode) or pulled by Ollama (local mode), then cached and reused offline. This transfers no page content, only the model files themselves.
    • WebLLM runtime files: in-browser mode also fetches the WebLLM library's own config/wasm assets from raw.githubusercontent.com, the same as the model weights above, no page content, just the runtime itself.
    • In-browser WASM runtime: Ask's local embedding model, and Firefox's Transformers.js summarization engine, load their shared ONNX WASM runtime once from cdn.jsdelivr.net (model weights come from Hugging Face, same as above), again the runtime only, never page content.
    • YouTube transcripts: on a YouTube page, the extractor fetches that video's caption track from YouTube/Google (the site you're already on) to feed the transcript to the model. It is restricted to genuine youtube.com/google.com/googlevideo.com hosts.
    • YouTube sponsor-segment lookup (SponsorBlock): when summarizing a YouTube video, Apogee asks the crowdsourced SponsorBlock API which parts of the video are sponsor reads/self-promo, so they can be stripped from the transcript. This uses SponsorBlock's privacy-preserving k-anonymity endpoint: only the first 4 hex characters of the SHA-256 hash of the video ID are sent (never the video ID, URL, or any page content), and the matching entry is picked out locally. If the lookup fails, a local phrase heuristic runs instead, with no network call at all.
    • That's it, there are no other external calls. (See the extension's content_security_policy.connect-src in manifest.json for the exact allow-list this is enforced against, and ALLOWED_OLLAMA_HOSTS in background/service-worker.js, which rejects any Local Ollama host setting that isn't plain http://127.0.0.1 or http://localhost.)

sentences.en: In-browser WASM runtime: Ask's local embedding model, and Firefox's Transformers.js summarization engine, load their shared ONNX WASM runtime once from cdn.jsdelivr.net (model weights come from Hugging Face, same as above), again the runtime only, never page content.

sentences.zh: YouTube 脚本：在 YouTube 页面上，提取器从 YouTube/Google（您已经访问的网站）获取视频的字幕轨道，以将脚本提供给模型。

sentences.en: This uses SponsorBlock's privacy-preserving k-anonymity endpoint: only the first 4 hex characters of the SHA-256 hash of the video ID are sent (never the video ID, URL, or any page content), and the matching entry is picked out locally.
sentences.zh: 这使用了 SponsorBlock 的隐私保护 k-匿名端点：仅发送视频 ID 的 SHA-256 哈希值的前 4 个十六进制字符（绝不是视频 ID、URL 或任何页面内容），并在本地挑选出匹配的条目。

sentences.en: If the lookup fails, a local phrase heuristic runs instead, with no network call at all.
sentences.zh: 如果查找失败，则会运行本地短语启发式，根本不进行网络调用。

blocks.html_en: • PDFs: PDF text extraction runs fully client-side using pdf.js bundled into the extension, the PDF is downloaded straight into the browser tab (using that tab's own network context) and parsed there. Only the extracted text is ever handed to the model; the file itself never passes through any other process.

blocks.html_en: • Local Ollama's CORS setting (OLLAMA_ORIGINS): for the extension to reach Ollama at all, Ollama must be told to accept requests from the extension's origin, see Advanced: Local Ollama Mode. This is a browser-enforced allow-list, not a data-transmission path, but be aware that setting it to a wildcard (chrome-extension://*) rather than your specific extension ID lets any installed extension talk to your local Ollama API, not just Apogee. Ollama itself still only binds to 127.0.0.1 by default regardless of this setting, so it's never reachable from your network either way, this only affects which browser extensions can call it.

sentences.zh: 这是浏览器强制执行的允许列表，而不是数据传输路径，但请注意，将其设置为通配符 (chrome-extension://*) 而不是您的特定扩展 ID，可以让任何已安装的扩展与您本地的 Ollama API 通信，而不仅仅是 Apogee。

sentences.en: Ollama itself still only binds to 127.0.0.1 by default regardless of this setting, so it's never reachable from your network either way, this only affects which browser extensions can call it.

blocks.html_en: • No Telemetry, Tracking, or Analytics: Apogee includes no Google Analytics, Mixpanel, crash-reporting SDKs, or telemetry of any kind. No usage data is collected.

blocks.html_en:
• What's stored on your device (and how to control it):
    • To make reopening the popup instant, Apogee caches summaries, suggested prompts, extracted page text (for articles), and your recent questions/answers in local extension storage (chrome.storage.local), never transmitted, capped in size, and keyed by a hash of the URL (so URLs with tokens aren't stored in plaintext keys).
    • Sensitive sites are never cached, pages on known webmail/messaging hosts (Gmail, Outlook, Proton Mail, Yahoo Mail, Google Messages, WhatsApp Web, Telegram Web, Slack, Discord, Microsoft Teams) are always treated as ephemeral, regardless of your setting.
    • Under Settings, Privacy, you can switch to "Don't save (this session only)" so nothing page-derived is written to disk, and "Clear cached summaries & page data" wipes all cached content on demand (your preferences are kept).

sentences.en: Sensitive sites are never cached, pages on known webmail/messaging hosts (Gmail, Outlook, Proton Mail, Yahoo Mail, Google Messages, WhatsApp Web, Telegram Web, Slack, Discord, Microsoft Teams) are always treated as ephemeral, regardless of your setting.
sentences.zh: 敏感网站永远不会被缓存，已知网络邮件/消息传递主机（Gmail、Outlook、Proton Mail、Yahoo Mail、Google Messages、WhatsApp Web、Telegram Web、Slack、Discord、Microsoft Teams）上的页面始终被视为短暂的，无论您的设置如何。

blocks.html_en: • Under Settings, Privacy, you can switch to "Don't save (this session only)" so nothing page-derived is written to disk, and "Clear cached summaries & page data" wipes all cached content on demand (your preferences are kept).

sentences.en: Under Settings, Privacy, you can switch to "Don't save (this session only)" so nothing page-derived is written to disk, and "Clear cached summaries & page data" wipes all cached content on demand (your preferences are kept).
sentences.zh: 在“设置”、“隐私”下，您可以切换到“不保存（仅限此会话）”，这样页面派生的任何内容都不会写入磁盘，并且“清除缓存的摘要和页面数据”会按需擦除所有缓存的内容（保留您的首选项）。

blocks.html_en:
• Browser Permission Sandboxing:
    • activeTab + scripting: Apogee cannot read your browsing history or inspect other open tabs. It reads the currently active tab only when you click "Summarize"/"Ask", right-click and choose "Summarize this page", or use the keyboard shortcut.
    • storage: Holds your preferences plus the local cache described above.
    • unlimitedStorage: Lifts the default quota on chrome.storage.local so the cached summaries/page text above aren't evicted under normal storage pressure, it does not grant access to anything beyond that cache.
    • offscreen (Chrome/Edge only): Runs the in-browser WebLLM engine in a hidden document, since a service worker can't access WebGPU directly. Not used, and not requested, in the Firefox build.
    • alarms: Schedules the housekeeping timers that close the idle in-browser model and clean up finished request buffers, these need to survive the extension's background worker being suspended between uses. No user data is involved.
    • clipboardWrite: Lets the popup's copy buttons (summary, answer, past summaries) write to your clipboard directly when you click them, instead of routing through an interactive browser permission prompt. Write-only, the extension can never read your clipboard's existing contents.
    • contextMenus: Adds the "Summarize this page" right-click entry. Doesn't grant any visibility into your browsing beyond the page you right-clicked on, which activeTab already covers.
    • notifications: Shows a local OS notification when a right-click/keyboard-shortcut-triggered summary finishes or fails, so you know it's ready without needing to keep the popup open. Purely local UI, no data leaves your device to show it.

content_html_zh:
由 WebGPU、WebAssembly 或您自己的本地 Ollama 提供支持的私有浏览器内 AI 摘要器。

Apogee 是一款用于文章、视频、电子邮件等的 AI 浏览器助手。 它完全在您的浏览器中运行：通过 WebGPU（Chrome、Edge 和其他 Chromium 浏览器）在 GPU 上运行，或者通过 WebAssembly (Firefox) 在 CPU 上运行。 没有后端、没有 API 密钥、没有云。 只需安装扩展即可。

对于高级用户，Apogee 还可以通过 127.0.0.1 直接连接到本地 Ollama 实例以运行更大的模型。

TL;DR：Apogee 是一款离线优先的私人 AI 助手，完全在浏览器中、Chromium 浏览器中的 WebGPU 上以及 Firefox 中的 WebAssembly 上运行，并且零云依赖性或 API 密钥。 它总结了文章、YouTube 视频和 PDF，并使用本地检索回答有关它们的问题，所有这些都具有完全的隐私性。 高级用户可以切换到本地 Ollama 模式，在自己的计算机上运行更大的模型，但仍然没有留下任何内容。 Apogee 被设计为完全本地化、尊重隐私的替代依赖于云的工具（例如 Mozilla 已停产的 Orbit）。

灵感：Orbit（被 Mozilla 杀死）

Apogee 的灵感来自于 Mozilla 已停止的 Orbit 项目（请阅读 Mozilla 对 Orbit 的评论）。 Orbit 尝试提供基于浏览器的页面摘要，但它依赖于集中式 API 服务器 (Mistral 7B) 并使用 store_result 等端点在服务器端缓存摘要。

Apogee 通过完全本地优先修复了 Orbit 的架构和隐私缺陷：

  • 零服务器开销：Apogee 不通过远程云 API 路由查询，而是通过 WebGPU 完全在设备上执行标记化和推理。
  • 无数据泄漏：Apogee 不会将页面内容或生成的摘要发送到任何外部端点，您的数据永远不会离开您的计算机。
  • 企业独立性：由于 Apogee 没有服务器依赖项或需要支付的云基础设施，因此它永远不会关闭或日落。

它是如何运作的

Apogee 直接在浏览器中运行量化语言模型。 在 Chrome、Edge 和其他 Chromium 浏览器上，它使用 WebLLM，它通过 WebGPU 在 GPU 上执行模型。 在 Firefox 上，它使用 Transformers.js，它通过 WebAssembly 在 CPU 上运行较小的 ONNX 模型，并在现代 CPU 上表现良好。 在这两种情况下，第一次使用都会下载模型权重（大约 270 MB 到 2.2 GB，具体取决于模型）并将其缓存在本地。 之后，一切都离线运行。

喜欢更大的型号？ 切换到本地 Ollama 模式，扩展程序将通过 127.0.0.1 直接与您自己的 Ollama 实例通信，无需安装或运行单独的后端。

Ask 比摘要本身更进一步：Apogee 不是盲目地将长页面截断到前几千个字符，而是在本地嵌入页面（一个小型​​设备上模型，与上面的 LLM 权重相同的信任层）并仅使用与您的问题最相关的段落进行回答。 这意味着询问隐藏在长文章、PDF 或视频记录中的内容仍然有效，而不仅仅是适合开头截断片段的内容。 （检索目前在 Chromium 浏览器上运行；Firefox 目前回退到简单的截断切片。）

通过页面突出显示，您可以根据来源检查摘要：单击任何项​​目符号（或行，在句子/段落模式下），Apogee 会找到原始页面的段落，它很可能是基于使用 Ask 使用的相同设备上检索，然后滚动到并在实时页面中突出显示它。 对于抽查索赔很有用，无需重新阅读整篇文章。 目前仅限 Chromium，与上面 Ask 的检索相同的限制。

快速入门

  1. 安装扩展（见下文）。
  2. 打开任意网页。
  3. 单击 Apogee 图标，然后总结此页面。
  4. 首次使用时，模型会自动下载。 之后就是即时的。

就是这样。 无需后端安装，无需终端命令。

更快的总结方法：右键单击页面上的任意位置并选择总结此页面，或使用键盘快捷键（默认 Alt+Shift+U，可在 chrome://extensions/shortcuts 重新映射）。 两者都可以在不打开弹出窗口的情况下工作。 系统通知让您知道它何时准备就绪；单击它（或打开弹出窗口）以查看结果。 如果您在弹出窗口仍在生成时打开它，它将显示正常的加载视图而不是默认主页。

使用 Apogee 的两种方法

Apogee 提供两种操作模式来平衡易用性和原始功能：

浏览器内 AI（Chrome/Edge 上的 WebLLM、Firefox 上的 Transformers.js） 当地奥拉马
模型大小：小型、快速模型（~270 MB 至 2.2 GB） 型号尺寸：更大、功能更强大的型号（4B 至 8B+）
设置：需要零设置；首次运行时自动下载 设置：需要安装 Ollama（无需运行单独的后端）
执行：通过 WebGPU (WebLLM) 或 WebAssembly (Transformers.js) 直接在浏览器中运行 执行：扩展直接与 Ollama 的 HTTP API 对话
离线：模型权重缓存后完全离线 离线：完全离线，通过 127.0.0.1 进行通信

支持的浏览器内模型

WebLLM（WebGPU、Chrome/Edge）

Model 下载大小 最适合
Qwen 2.5 1.5B（默认） ~900 MB 多语言摘要
斯摩尔LM2 1.7B ~1 GB 一般任务
火焰3.2 1B ~700 MB 轻量、快速
Φ3.5迷你 ~2.2 GB 更强的推理能力

Transformers.js（WASM/CPU，仅限 Firefox）

Model 下载大小 最适合
SmolLM2 360M（默认） ~270 MB 最小/最快、快速摘要
Qwen 2.5 0.5B ~480 MB 多种语言
火焰3.2 1B ~1.2 GB 推理能力更强，CPU 速度更慢

通过 Transformers.js（WASM 后端上的 ONNX 模型）运行。 选择它是因为它永远不会生成 Worker（onnxruntime-web 的代理模式被硬编码关闭），这与基于 WebGPU 的 WebLLM（需要 Firefox 没有的屏幕外文档）或 wllama（需要 blob:-URL Worker，Chrome 和 Firefox 的扩展 CSP 都阻止）不同。 生成是单线程的（扩展页面不是跨源隔离的，因此没有 SharedArrayBuffer），并且上下文的上限为 4096 个令牌，以保持 CPU 上的合理延迟。 在现代/快速 CPU 上，这仍然可以很好地概括默认的 SmolLM2 360M 模型；在较旧或低功耗的硬件上，预计生成速度会明显变慢，并考虑改用 Local Ollama。

支持的 Ollama 型号

您提取的任何模型都会自动显示在扩展程序的设置中（请参阅高级：本地 Ollama 模式）。 如果您还没有提取任何内容，那么这些只是一个起点：

Model Size 拉取命令 推荐用于
Gemma 3 ~4B 奥拉马拉杰玛3:4b 出色的轻量级任务
Qwen 3 8B ~8B 奥拉马拉qwen3:8b 多轮聊天与推理
米斯特拉尔最新 ~7B 奥拉马·拉·米斯特拉尔：最新 通用语言能力
呼叫 3.1 8B ~8B llama 拉 llama3.1:8b 一般推理和编码

汇总还会根据您选择的模型调整其分块：较大上下文的模型（例如 llama3.1、qwen2.5、gemma3）会获得更大的块和更少的长内容传递，而不是相同的固定块大小，无论模型实际可以处理什么。

浏览器支持

Apogee 提供两个版本：Chromium 版本（dist/chrome、Manifest V3，带有用于 WebGPU 的离屏文档）和 Firefox 版本（dist/firefox，无离屏文档）。 任何基于 Chromium 的东西都接受相同的构建。

火狐浏览器 140+ No 是（默认） Yes Firefox 的 WebExtensions 实现没有 browser.offscreen API，WebLLM 需要该 API 在可见选项卡之外运行 WebGPU（服务工作人员无法直接访问 WebGPU）。 Transformers.js 既不需要 WebGPU，也不需要 Worker，因此它直接在 Firefox 的后台页面中运行，并且是默认的浏览器内提供程序。 Firefox 版本声明 strict_min_version: 140.0（清单的 data_collection_permissions 键需要），较旧的 Firefox 将拒绝安装它而不是默默地失败。
狩猎之旅 No No Apogee 目前不构建或发布 Safari 扩展（与 Chrome/Firefox 不同的打包工具链）；无论 Safari 自己的 WebGPU 支持如何，都不会进行评估

请参阅 MDN 的 WebGPU API 浏览器兼容性表，了解每个浏览器/每个操作系统的 WebGPU 版本的确切支持，它是一个快速移动的目标，也是比此处硬编码的数字更好的事实来源。 浏览器内 (WebLLM) 模式特别需要支持 WebGPU 的 GPU（过去几年的大多数 GPU）。 除了 Ollama 本身的需求之外，本地 Ollama 模式本身没有任何 GPU 要求。

安装扩展

Chrome、Edge、Brave、Opera、Vivaldi、Arc、Dia

这些都是基于 Chromium 的，并使用相同的 dist/chrome 构建和加载步骤；仅扩展页面 URL 略有不同（chrome://extensions、edge://extensions、brave://extensions、dia://extensions/ 等）。

  1. 从 Releases 下载打包的扩展 .zip。
  2. 在您的计算机上提取/解压缩下载的 .zip 文件。
  3. 打开浏览器的扩展程序页面（Chrome/Brave/Opera/Vivaldi 上为 chrome://extensions，Edge 上为 edge://extensions，Dia 上为 dia://extensions/）。
  4. 启用开发者模式（在右上角切换）。
  5. 单击“加载已解压的文件”并选择解压缩的文件夹（包含manifest.json，而不是ZIP 文件本身）。

从源代码构建（开发人员选项）

  1. 克隆这个存储库。
  2. cd apogee-extension && npm install && npm run build
  3. 转到浏览器的扩展页面并启用开发人员模式。
  4. 单击加载解压文件并选择 apogee-extension/dist/chrome 文件夹。 npm run build 会生成 dist/chrome 和 dist/firefox。 对于任何基于 Chromium 的浏览器使用 dist/chrome，对于 Firefox 使用 dist/firefox。 您还可以使用 npm run build:chrome 或 npm run build:firefox 构建单个目标。

火狐浏览器

您可以直接从 Mozilla Add-ons 安装 Apogee 或从 Releases 下载软件包。

Firefox 开箱即用：浏览器内 AI 通过 WebAssembly 上的 Transformers.js 运行（默认为 SmolLM2 360M，无需设置）。 在较旧或低功耗的 CPU 上，生成速度可能会很慢，请在设置中切换到本地 Ollama 模式，以便使用较大的模型获得更快的结果。

高级：本地 Ollama 模式

如果您更喜欢通过 Ollama 在本地运行较大的模型 (8B+)，Apogee 可以直接通过 HTTP 与其进行对话；无需安装或保持运行单独的后端服务器。

1. 安装奥拉马

从 https://ollama.com 安装，然后拉取您想要的模型：

ollama pull gemma3:4b   # and qwen3:8b, mistral:latest, llama3.1:8b

2. 允许分机到达 Ollama (CORS)

Ollama 仅接受来自允许列表中的一组来源的浏览器发起的请求，并且 chrome-extension:// 不在该默认列表中。 在启动 Ollama 之前设置 OLLAMA_ORIGINS：

OLLAMA_ORIGINS="chrome-extension://<your-extension-id>" ollama serve

在 chrome://extensions 上查找 <your-extension-id>（开发者模式已开启）。

如果 Ollama 作为系统服务运行（例如通过 Linux 上的包管理器安装），请将其设置为持久覆盖，而不是在命令行上传递它：

sudo mkdir -p /etc/systemd/system/ollama.service.d
printf '[Service]\nEnvironment="OLLAMA_ORIGINS=chrome-extension://<your-extension-id>"\n' \
  | sudo tee /etc/systemd/system/ollama.service.d/override.conf
sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl restart ollama
安全说明：OLLAMA_ORIGINS=chrome-extension://*（允许每个扩展）在开发时很方便，但这意味着浏览器中安装的任何其他扩展也可以访问本地 Ollama 实例的 API。 将其范围限定为您的特定扩展 ID，以进行快速本地测试之外的任何操作。

3. 将延伸部分指向 Ollama

打开扩展程序，转到“设置”，然后选择“本地 Ollama”。 主机字段默认为 http://127.0.0.1:11434（Ollama 自己的默认端口），仅当您已将 Ollama 配置为在其他地方侦听时才更改它。

连接后，模型列表将根据您实际拉取的内容（通过 Ollama 自己的 /api/tags）实时填充，而不是固定列表，因此您拉取的任何模型都会自动显示。 如果还无法访问 Ollama，则会显示一个小的默认列表，以便您仍然可以在启动之前选择一个模型。

就是这样：没有远地点后端，没有单独的服务器进程需要管理。

性能基准

浏览器内人工智能 (WebGPU)

  • 生成吞吐量：~30 至 50 个令牌/秒（取决于 GPU）
  • 模型冷加载：~1 到 3 秒（一旦缓存在浏览器存储中）
  • 首次运行缓存下载：约 1 到 3 分钟，具体取决于网络带宽（下载约 700 MB 到 2.2 GB 模型权重）

当地奥拉马

在 Apple M2（gemma3:4b，通过 Metal 的 GPU）上本地测量：

公制 Value
发电量 ~73 个令牌/秒
型号冷负荷 ~0.25 s
短页/问题 端到端约 1 至 1.5 秒
长页面（~40k 字符，多块） 第一颗子弹在约 2 秒内发出，总共约 12 秒

隐私和权限

隐私是 Apogee 的核心支柱。 关键保证很简单：您的页面内容以及从中生成的摘要/答案永远不会发送到任何云服务或第三方。 推理发生在您自己的设备 (WebGPU) 或您自己的计算机 (127.0.0.1 Ollama) 上。 下面的详细信息是关于确实发生的少数网络请求以及磁盘上保存的内容的详细信息。

  • 推理发生的位置：浏览器内模式：标记化和推理完全在本地设备上、通过 WebGPU（WebLLM、Chrome/Edge）在 GPU 上运行或通过 WebAssembly（Transformers.js、Firefox）在 CPU 上运行。 您的页面内容和摘要永远不会传输到任何地方。 本地 Ollama 模式：页面内容仅通过本地环回 (127.0.0.1) 直接传输到您自己的 Ollama 实例的 HTTP API，而不会传输到云。 路径中没有中间后端进程，扩展是 Ollama 的唯一客户端跃点。
  • Apogee 发出的唯一出站网络请求：模型权重从 Hugging Face（浏览器内模式）下载一次或由 Ollama 拉取（本地模式），然后缓存并离线重用。 这不传输页面内容，仅传输模型文件本身。 WebLLM 运行时文件：浏览器内模式还会从 raw.githubusercontent.com 获取 WebLLM 库自己的配置/wasm 资产，与上面的模型权重相同，没有页面内容，只有运行时本身。 浏览器内 WASM 运行时：Ask 的本地嵌入模型和 Firefox 的 Transformers.js 摘要引擎，从 cdn.jsdelivr.net 加载其共享的 ONNX WASM 运行时（模型权重来自 Hugging Face，与上面相同），同样仅运行时，而不是页面内容。 YouTube 脚本：在 YouTube 页面上，提取器从 YouTube/Google（您已经访问的网站）获取视频的字幕轨道，以将脚本提供给模型。 它仅限于真正的 youtube.com/google.com/googlevideo.com 主机。 YouTube 赞助商细分查找 (SponsorBlock)：在总结 YouTube 视频时，Apogee 会询问众包 SponsorBlock API 视频的哪些部分是赞助商阅读/自我宣传，以便可以将它们从脚本中删除。 这使用了 SponsorBlock 的隐私保护 k-匿名端点：仅发送视频 ID 的 SHA-256 哈希值的前 4 个十六进制字符（绝不是视频 ID、URL 或任何页面内容），并在本地挑选出匹配的条目。 如果查找失败，则会运行本地短语启发式，根本不进行网络调用。 就这样，没有其他外部调用。 （请参阅manifest.json中扩展程序的content_security_policy.connect-src，了解强制执行的确切允许列表，以及background/service-worker.js中的ALLOWED_OLLAMA_HOSTS，它拒绝任何非普通http://127.0.0.1或http://localhost的本地Ollama主机设置。）
  • PDF：PDF 文本提取使用捆绑到扩展中的 pdf.js 完全在客户端运行，PDF 直接下载到浏览器选项卡中（使用该选项卡自己的网络上下文）并在那里进行解析。 只有提取的文本才会传递给模型；文件本身从不经过任何其他进程。
  • 本地 Ollama 的 CORS 设置 (OLLAMA_ORIGINS)：为了让扩展程序完全到达 Ollama，必须告知 Ollama 接受来自扩展程序源的请求，请参阅高级：本地 Ollama 模式。 这是浏览器强制执行的允许列表，而不是数据传输路径，但请注意，将其设置为通配符 (chrome-extension://*) 而不是您的特定扩展 ID，可以让任何已安装的扩展与您本地的 Ollama API 通信，而不仅仅是 Apogee。 无论此设置如何，Ollama 本身默认情况下仍然只绑定到 127.0.0.1，因此无论哪种方式都无法从您的网络访问它，这只会影响哪些浏览器扩展可以调用它。
  • 无遥测、跟踪或分析：Apogee 不包含 Google Analytics、Mixpanel、崩溃报告 SDK 或任何类型的遥测。 不收集使用数据。
  • 设备上存储的内容（以及如何控制它）：为了立即重新打开弹出窗口，Apogee 会缓存摘要、建议的提示、提取的页面文本（针对文章）以及本地扩展存储 (chrome.storage.local) 中最近的问题/答案，这些内容从不传输，大小有上限，并由 URL 的哈希值作为键控（因此带有令牌的 URL 不会存储在明文键中）。 敏感网站永远不会被缓存，已知网络邮件/消息传递主机（Gmail、Outlook、Proton Mail、Yahoo Mail、Google Messages、WhatsApp Web、Telegram Web、Slack、Discord、Microsoft Teams）上的页面始终被视为短暂的，无论您的设置如何。 在“设置”、“隐私”下，您可以切换到“不保存（仅限此会话）”，这样页面派生的任何内容都不会写入磁盘，并且“清除缓存的摘要和页面数据”会按需擦除所有缓存的内容（保留您的首选项）。
  • 浏览器权限沙盒：activeTab + 脚本：Apogee 无法读取您的浏览历史记录或检查其他打开的选项卡。 仅当您单击“摘要”/“询问”、右键单击并选择“摘要此页”或使用键盘快捷键时，它才会读取当前活动的选项卡。 存储：保存您的首选项以及上述本地缓存。 unlimitedStorage：提升 chrome.storage.local 上的默认配额，以便在正常存储压力下不会驱逐上面缓存的摘要/页面文本，它不会授予对缓存之外的任何内容的访问权限。 离屏（仅限 Chrome/Edge）：在隐藏文档中运行浏览器内 WebLLM 引擎，因为 Service Worker 无法直接访问 WebGPU。 在 Firefox 版本中未使用，也未请求。 警报：安排内务计时器关闭空闲的浏览器内模型并清理已完成的请求缓冲区，这些需要在扩展程序的后台工作人员在使用之间暂停时继续存在。 不涉及用户数据。 ClipboardWrite：让弹出窗口的复制按钮（摘要、答案、过去的摘要）在单击时直接写入剪贴板，而不是通过交互式浏览器权限提示进行路由。 只写，扩展程序永远无法读取剪贴板的现有内容。 contextMenus：添加“摘要此页”右键单击条目。 除了您右键单击的页面（activeTab 已经涵盖了该页面）之外，不会对您的浏览情况提供任何可见性。 通知：当右键单击/键盘快捷键触发的摘要完成或失败时，显示本地操作系统通知，以便您知道它已准备就绪，而无需保持弹出窗口打开。 纯本地 UI，没有数据离开您的设备来显示。
  • 模型权重存储在本地标准浏览器缓存结构中，并且从不传输。
